<article class="bd-article" role="main">
<section id="langchain-gallery">
<h1>LangChain Gallery<a class="headerlink" href="#langchain-gallery" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h1>
<p>Lots of people have built some pretty awesome stuff with LangChain.
This is a collection of our favorites.
If you see any other demos that you think we should highlight, be sure to let us know!</p>
<section id="open-source">
<h2>Open Source<a class="headerlink" href="#open-source" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<div class="sphinx-bs container pb-4 docutils">
<div class="row docutils">
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/bborn/howdoi.ai"><span>HowDoI.ai</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">This is an experiment in building a large-language-model-backed chatbot. It can hold a conversation, remember previous comments/questions,
and answer all types of queries (history, web search, movie data, weather, news, and more).</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1sKSTjt9cPstl_WMZ86JsgEqFG-aSAwkn?usp=sharing"><span>YouTube Transcription QA with Sources</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">An end-to-end example of doing question answering on YouTube transcripts, returning the timestamps as sources to legitimize the answer.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/normandmickey/MrsStax"><span>QA Slack Bot</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">This application is a Slack Bot that uses Langchain and OpenAI’s GPT3 language model to provide domain specific answers. You provide the documents.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/OpenBioLink/ThoughtSource"><span>ThoughtSource</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">A central, open resource and community around data and tools related to chain-of-thought reasoning in large language models.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/blackhc/llm-strategy"><span>LLM Strategy</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">This Python package adds a decorator llm_strategy that connects to an LLM (such as OpenAI’s GPT-3) and uses the LLM to “implement” abstract methods in interface classes. It does this by forwarding requests to the LLM and converting the responses back to Python data using Python’s @dataclasses.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/JohnNay/llm-lobbyist"><span>Zero-Shot Corporate Lobbyist</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">A notebook showing how to use GPT to help with the work of a corporate lobbyist.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://dagster.io/blog/chatgpt-langchain"><span>Dagster Documentation ChatBot</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">A jupyter notebook demonstrating how you could create a semantic search engine on documents in one of your Google Folders</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/venuv/langchain_semantic_search"><span>Google Folder Semantic Search</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Build a GitHub support bot with GPT3, LangChain, and Python.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://huggingface.co/spaces/team7/talk_with_wind"><span>Talk With Wind</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Record sounds of anything (birds, wind, fire, train station) and chat with it.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://huggingface.co/spaces/JavaFXpert/Chat-GPT-LangChain"><span>ChatGPT LangChain</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">This simple application demonstrates a conversational agent implemented with OpenAI GPT-3.5 and LangChain. When necessary, it leverages tools for complex math, searching the internet, and accessing news and weather.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://huggingface.co/spaces/JavaFXpert/gpt-math-techniques"><span>GPT Math Techniques</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">A Hugging Face spaces project showing off the benefits of using PAL for math problems.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1xt2IsFPGYMEQdoJFNgWNAjWGxa60VXdV"><span>GPT Political Compass</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Measure the political compass of GPT.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/hwchase17/notion-qa"><span>Notion Database Question-Answering Bot</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Open source GitHub project shows how to use LangChain to create a chatbot that can answer questions about an arbitrary Notion database.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/jerryjliu/llama_index"><span>LlamaIndex</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">LlamaIndex (formerly GPT Index) is a project consisting of a set of data structures that are created using GPT-3 and can be traversed using GPT-3 in order to answer queries.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/JavaFXpert/llm-grovers-search-party"><span>Grover’s Algorithm</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Leveraging Qiskit, OpenAI and LangChain to demonstrate Grover’s algorithm</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://huggingface.co/spaces/rituthombre/QNim"><span>QNimGPT</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">A chat UI to play Nim, where a player can select an opponent, either a quantum computer or an AI</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://colab.research.google.com/drive/19WTIWC3prw5LDMHmRMvqNV2loD9FHls6?usp=sharing"><span>ReAct TextWorld</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Leveraging the ReActTextWorldAgent to play TextWorld with an LLM!</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/jagilley/fact-checker"><span>Fact Checker</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">This repo is a simple demonstration of using LangChain to do fact-checking with prompt chaining.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/arc53/docsgpt"><span>DocsGPT</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Answer questions about the documentation of any project</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<section id="misc-colab-notebooks">
<h3>Misc. Colab Notebooks<a class="headerlink" href="#misc-colab-notebooks" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h3>
<div class="sphinx-bs container pb-4 docutils">
<div class="row docutils">

<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1AAyEdTz-Z6ShKvewbt1ZHUICqak0MiwR?usp=sharing"><span>Wolfram Alpha in Conversational Agent</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Give ChatGPT a WolframAlpha neural implant</p>

</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">

<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">

</section>
<section id="proprietary">
<h2>Proprietary<a class="headerlink" href="#proprietary" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<div class="sphinx-bs container pb-4 docutils">
<div class="row docutils">
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://twitter.com/sjwhitmore/status/1580593217153531908?s=20&amp;t=neQvtZZTlp623U3LZwz3bQ"><span>Daimon</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">A chat-based AI personal assistant with long-term memory about you.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://twitter.com/dory111111/status/1608406234646052870?s=20&amp;t=XYlrbKM0ornJsrtGa0br-g"><span>AI Assisted SQL Query Generator</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">An app to write SQL using natural language, and execute against real DB.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://twitter.com/krrish_dh/status/1581028925618106368?s=20&amp;t=neQvtZZTlp623U3LZwz3bQ"><span>Clerkie</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Stack Tracing QA Bot to help debug complex stack tracing (especially the ones that go multi-function/file deep).</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://twitter.com/Raza_Habib496/status/1596880140490838017?s=20&amp;t=6MqEQYWfSqmJwsKahjCVOA"><span>Sales Email Writer</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">By Raza Habib, this demo utilizes LangChain + SerpAPI + HumanLoop to write sales emails. Give it a company name and a person, this application will use Google Search (via SerpAPI) to get more information on the company and the person, and then write them a sales message.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://twitter.com/chillzaza_/status/1592961099384905730?s=20&amp;t=EhU8jl0KyCPJ7vE9Rnz-cQ"><span>Question-Answering on a Web Browser</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">By Zahid Khawaja, this demo utilizes question answering to answer questions about a given website. A followup added this for <a class="reference external" href="https://twitter.com/chillzaza_/status/1593739682013220865?s=20&amp;t=EhU8jl0KyCPJ7vE9Rnz-cQ">YouTube videos</a>, and then another followup added it for <a class="reference external" href="https://twitter.com/chillzaza_/status/1594847151238037505?s=20&amp;t=EhU8jl0KyCPJ7vE9Rnz-cQ">Wikipedia</a>.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://mynd.so"><span>Mynd</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">A journaling app for self-care that uses AI to uncover insights and patterns over time.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</section>
</section>

<article class="bd-article" role="main">
<section id="langchain-gallery">
<h1>LangChain Gallery<a class="headerlink" href="#langchain-gallery" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h1>
<p>Lots of people have built some pretty awesome stuff with LangChain.
This is a collection of our favorites.
If you see any other demos that you think we should highlight, be sure to let us know!</p>
<section id="open-source">
<h2>Open Source<a class="headerlink" href="#open-source" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<div class="sphinx-bs container pb-4 docutils">
<div class="row docutils">
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/bborn/howdoi.ai"><span>HowDoI.ai</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">This is an experiment in building a large-language-model-backed chatbot. It can hold a conversation, remember previous comments/questions,
and answer all types of queries (history, web search, movie data, weather, news, and more).</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1sKSTjt9cPstl_WMZ86JsgEqFG-aSAwkn?usp=sharing"><span>YouTube Transcription QA with Sources</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">An end-to-end example of doing question answering on YouTube transcripts, returning the timestamps as sources to legitimize the answer.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/normandmickey/MrsStax"><span>QA Slack Bot</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">This application is a Slack Bot that uses Langchain and OpenAI’s GPT3 language model to provide domain specific answers. You provide the documents.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/OpenBioLink/ThoughtSource"><span>ThoughtSource</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">A central, open resource and community around data and tools related to chain-of-thought reasoning in large language models.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/blackhc/llm-strategy"><span>LLM Strategy</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">This Python package adds a decorator llm_strategy that connects to an LLM (such as OpenAI’s GPT-3) and uses the LLM to “implement” abstract methods in interface classes. It does this by forwarding requests to the LLM and converting the responses back to Python data using Python’s @dataclasses.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/JohnNay/llm-lobbyist"><span>Zero-Shot Corporate Lobbyist</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">A notebook showing how to use GPT to help with the work of a corporate lobbyist.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://dagster.io/blog/chatgpt-langchain"><span>Dagster Documentation ChatBot</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">A jupyter notebook demonstrating how you could create a semantic search engine on documents in one of your Google Folders</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/venuv/langchain_semantic_search"><span>Google Folder Semantic Search</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Build a GitHub support bot with GPT3, LangChain, and Python.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://huggingface.co/spaces/team7/talk_with_wind"><span>Talk With Wind</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Record sounds of anything (birds, wind, fire, train station) and chat with it.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://huggingface.co/spaces/JavaFXpert/Chat-GPT-LangChain"><span>ChatGPT LangChain</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">This simple application demonstrates a conversational agent implemented with OpenAI GPT-3.5 and LangChain. When necessary, it leverages tools for complex math, searching the internet, and accessing news and weather.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://huggingface.co/spaces/JavaFXpert/gpt-math-techniques"><span>GPT Math Techniques</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">A Hugging Face spaces project showing off the benefits of using PAL for math problems.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1xt2IsFPGYMEQdoJFNgWNAjWGxa60VXdV"><span>GPT Political Compass</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Measure the political compass of GPT.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/hwchase17/notion-qa"><span>Notion Database Question-Answering Bot</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Open source GitHub project shows how to use LangChain to create a chatbot that can answer questions about an arbitrary Notion database.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/jerryjliu/llama_index"><span>LlamaIndex</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">LlamaIndex (formerly GPT Index) is a project consisting of a set of data structures that are created using GPT-3 and can be traversed using GPT-3 in order to answer queries.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/JavaFXpert/llm-grovers-search-party"><span>Grover’s Algorithm</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Leveraging Qiskit, OpenAI and LangChain to demonstrate Grover’s algorithm</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://huggingface.co/spaces/rituthombre/QNim"><span>QNimGPT</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">A chat UI to play Nim, where a player can select an opponent, either a quantum computer or an AI</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://colab.research.google.com/drive/19WTIWC3prw5LDMHmRMvqNV2loD9FHls6?usp=sharing"><span>ReAct TextWorld</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Leveraging the ReActTextWorldAgent to play TextWorld with an LLM!</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://github.com/jagilley/fact-checker"><span>Fact Checker</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">This repo is a simple demonstration of using LangChain to do fact-checking with prompt chaining.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">

<section id="proprietary">
<h2>Proprietary<a class="headerlink" href="#proprietary" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<div class="sphinx-bs container pb-4 docutils">
<div class="row docutils">
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://twitter.com/sjwhitmore/status/1580593217153531908?s=20&amp;t=neQvtZZTlp623U3LZwz3bQ"><span>Daimon</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">A chat-based AI personal assistant with long-term memory about you.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://twitter.com/dory111111/status/1608406234646052870?s=20&amp;t=XYlrbKM0ornJsrtGa0br-g"><span>AI Assisted SQL Query Generator</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">An app to write SQL using natural language, and execute against real DB.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://twitter.com/krrish_dh/status/1581028925618106368?s=20&amp;t=neQvtZZTlp623U3LZwz3bQ"><span>Clerkie</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">Stack Tracing QA Bot to help debug complex stack tracing (especially the ones that go multi-function/file deep).</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://twitter.com/Raza_Habib496/status/1596880140490838017?s=20&amp;t=6MqEQYWfSqmJwsKahjCVOA"><span>Sales Email Writer</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">By Raza Habib, this demo utilizes LangChain + SerpAPI + HumanLoop to write sales emails. Give it a company name and a person, this application will use Google Search (via SerpAPI) to get more information on the company and the person, and then write them a sales message.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://twitter.com/chillzaza_/status/1592961099384905730?s=20&amp;t=EhU8jl0KyCPJ7vE9Rnz-cQ"><span>Question-Answering on a Web Browser</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">By Zahid Khawaja, this demo utilizes question answering to answer questions about a given website. A followup added this for <a class="reference external" href="https://twitter.com/chillzaza_/status/1593739682013220865?s=20&amp;t=EhU8jl0KyCPJ7vE9Rnz-cQ">YouTube videos</a>, and then another followup added it for <a class="reference external" href="https://twitter.com/chillzaza_/status/1594847151238037505?s=20&amp;t=EhU8jl0KyCPJ7vE9Rnz-cQ">Wikipedia</a>.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="d-flex col-lg-6 col-md-6 col-sm-6 col-xs-12 p-2 docutils">
<div class="card w-100 shadow docutils">
<div class="card-body text-center docutils">
<p class="card-text"><a class="sphinx-bs btn text-wrap stretched-link btn-lg reference external" href="https://mynd.so"><span>Mynd</span></a></p>
</div>
<div class="card-footer docutils">
<p class="card-text">A journaling app for self-care that uses AI to uncover insights and patterns over time.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</section>
</section>

</html># Glossary

This is a collection of terminology commonly used when developing LLM applications.
It contains reference to external papers or sources where the concept was first introduced,
as well as to places in LangChain where the concept is used.

## Chain of Thought Prompting

A prompting technique used to encourage the model to generate a series of intermediate reasoning steps.
A less formal way to induce this behavior is to include “Let’s think step-by-step” in the prompt.

Resources:

- [Chain-of-Thought Paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2201.11903.pdf)
- [Step-by-Step Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.00114)

## Action Plan Generation

A prompt usage that uses a language model to generate actions to take.
The results of these actions can then be fed back into the language model to generate a subsequent action.

Resources:

- [WebGPT Paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2112.09332.pdf)
- [SayCan Paper](https://say-can.github.io/assets/palm_saycan.pdf)

## ReAct Prompting

A prompting technique that combines Chain-of-Thought prompting with action plan generation.
This induces the to model to think about what action to take, then take it.

Resources:

- [Paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2210.03629.pdf)
- [LangChain Example](modules/agents/agents/examples/react.ipynb)

## Self-ask

A prompting method that builds on top of chain-of-thought prompting.
In this method, the model explicitly asks itself follow-up questions, which are then answered by an external search engine.

Resources:

- [Paper](https://ofir.io/self-ask.pdf)
- [LangChain Example](modules/agents/agents/examples/self_ask_with_search.ipynb)

## Prompt Chaining

Combining multiple LLM calls together, with the output of one-step being the input to the next.

Resources:

- [PromptChainer Paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.06566.pdf)
- [Language Model Cascades](https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.10342)
- [ICE Primer Book](https://primer.ought.org/)
- [Socratic Models](https://socraticmodels.github.io/)

## Memetic Proxy

Encouraging the LLM to respond in a certain way framing the discussion in a context that the model knows of and that will result in that type of response. For example, as a conversation between a student and a teacher.

Resources:

- [Paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2102.07350.pdf)

## Self Consistency

A decoding strategy that samples a diverse set of reasoning paths and then selects the most consistent answer.
Is most effective when combined with Chain-of-thought prompting.

Resources:

- [Paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.11171.pdf)

## Inception

Also called “First Person Instruction”.
Encouraging the model to think a certain way by including the start of the model’s response in the prompt.

Resources:

- [Example](https://twitter.com/goodside/status/1583262455207460865?s=20&amp;t=8Hz7XBnK1OF8siQrxxCIGQ)

## MemPrompt

MemPrompt maintains a memory of errors and user feedback, and uses them to prevent repetition of mistakes.

Resources:

- [Paper](https://memprompt.com/)<!DOCTYPE html>

<h1>Glossary</h1>
<!-- Table of contents -->
<div id="print-main-content">
<div id="jb-print-toc">
<div>
<h2> Contents </h2>
</div>
<nav aria-label="Page">
<ul class="visible nav section-nav flex-column">
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#chain-of-thought-prompting">Chain of Thought Prompting</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#action-plan-generation">Action Plan Generation</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#react-prompting">ReAct Prompting</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#self-ask">Self-ask</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#prompt-chaining">Prompt Chaining</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#memetic-proxy">Memetic Proxy</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#self-consistency">Self Consistency</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#inception">Inception</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#memprompt">MemPrompt</a></li>
</ul>
</nav>

<article class="bd-article" role="main">
<section id="glossary">
<h1>Glossary<a class="headerlink" href="#glossary" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h1>
<p>This is a collection of terminology commonly used when developing LLM applications.
It contains reference to external papers or sources where the concept was first introduced,
as well as to places in LangChain where the concept is used.</p>
<section id="chain-of-thought-prompting">
<h2>Chain of Thought Prompting<a class="headerlink" href="#chain-of-thought-prompting" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting technique used to encourage the model to generate a series of intermediate reasoning steps.
A less formal way to induce this behavior is to include “Let’s think step-by-step” in the prompt.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2201.11903.pdf">Chain-of-Thought Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.00114">Step-by-Step Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="action-plan-generation">
<h2>Action Plan Generation<a class="headerlink" href="#action-plan-generation" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompt usage that uses a language model to generate actions to take.
The results of these actions can then be fed back into the language model to generate a subsequent action.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2112.09332.pdf">WebGPT Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://say-can.github.io/assets/palm_saycan.pdf">SayCan Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="react-prompting">
<h2>ReAct Prompting<a class="headerlink" href="#react-prompting" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting technique that combines Chain-of-Thought prompting with action plan generation.
This induces the to model to think about what action to take, then take it.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2210.03629.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference internal" href="modules/agents/agents/examples/react.html"><span class="doc std std-doc">LangChain Example</span></a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="self-ask">
<h2>Self-ask<a class="headerlink" href="#self-ask" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting method that builds on top of chain-of-thought prompting.
In this method, the model explicitly asks itself follow-up questions, which are then answered by an external search engine.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://ofir.io/self-ask.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference internal" href="modules/agents/agents/examples/self_ask_with_search.html"><span class="doc std std-doc">LangChain Example</span></a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="prompt-chaining">
<h2>Prompt Chaining<a class="headerlink" href="#prompt-chaining" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Combining multiple LLM calls together, with the output of one-step being the input to the next.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.06566.pdf">PromptChainer Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.10342">Language Model Cascades</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://primer.ought.org/">ICE Primer Book</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://socraticmodels.github.io/">Socratic Models</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="memetic-proxy">
<h2>Memetic Proxy<a class="headerlink" href="#memetic-proxy" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Encouraging the LLM to respond in a certain way framing the discussion in a context that the model knows of and that will result in that type of response. For example, as a conversation between a student and a teacher.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2102.07350.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="self-consistency">
<h2>Self Consistency<a class="headerlink" href="#self-consistency" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A decoding strategy that samples a diverse set of reasoning paths and then selects the most consistent answer.
Is most effective when combined with Chain-of-thought prompting.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.11171.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="inception">
<h2>Inception<a class="headerlink" href="#inception" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Also called “First Person Instruction”.
Encouraging the model to think a certain way by including the start of the model’s response in the prompt.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://twitter.com/goodside/status/1583262455207460865?s=20&amp;t=8Hz7XBnK1OF8siQrxxCIGQ">Example</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="memprompt">
<h2>MemPrompt<a class="headerlink" href="#memprompt" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>MemPrompt maintains a memory of errors and user feedback, and uses them to prevent repetition of mistakes.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://memprompt.com/">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
</section>
</article>

<h1>Glossary</h1>
<!-- Table of contents -->
<div id="print-main-content">
<div id="jb-print-toc">
<div>
<h2> Contents </h2>
</div>
<nav aria-label="Page">
<ul class="visible nav section-nav flex-column">
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#chain-of-thought-prompting">Chain of Thought Prompting</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#action-plan-generation">Action Plan Generation</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#react-prompting">ReAct Prompting</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#self-ask">Self-ask</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#prompt-chaining">Prompt Chaining</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#memetic-proxy">Memetic Proxy</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#self-consistency">Self Consistency</a></li>

</ul>

<article class="bd-article" role="main">
<section id="glossary">
<h1>Glossary<a class="headerlink" href="#glossary" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h1>
<p>This is a collection of terminology commonly used when developing LLM applications.
It contains reference to external papers or sources where the concept was first introduced,
as well as to places in LangChain where the concept is used.</p>
<section id="chain-of-thought-prompting">
<h2>Chain of Thought Prompting<a class="headerlink" href="#chain-of-thought-prompting" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting technique used to encourage the model to generate a series of intermediate reasoning steps.
A less formal way to induce this behavior is to include “Let’s think step-by-step” in the prompt.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2201.11903.pdf">Chain-of-Thought Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.00114">Step-by-Step Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="action-plan-generation">
<h2>Action Plan Generation<a class="headerlink" href="#action-plan-generation" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompt usage that uses a language model to generate actions to take.
The results of these actions can then be fed back into the language model to generate a subsequent action.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2112.09332.pdf">WebGPT Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://say-can.github.io/assets/palm_saycan.pdf">SayCan Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="react-prompting">
<h2>ReAct Prompting<a class="headerlink" href="#react-prompting" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting technique that combines Chain-of-Thought prompting with action plan generation.
This induces the to model to think about what action to take, then take it.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2210.03629.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference internal" href="modules/agents/agents/examples/react.html"><span class="doc std std-doc">LangChain Example</span></a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="self-ask">
<h2>Self-ask<a class="headerlink" href="#self-ask" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting method that builds on top of chain-of-thought prompting.
In this method, the model explicitly asks itself follow-up questions, which are then answered by an external search engine.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://ofir.io/self-ask.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference internal" href="modules/agents/agents/examples/self_ask_with_search.html"><span class="doc std std-doc">LangChain Example</span></a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="prompt-chaining">
<h2>Prompt Chaining<a class="headerlink" href="#prompt-chaining" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Combining multiple LLM calls together, with the output of one-step being the input to the next.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.06566.pdf">PromptChainer Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.10342">Language Model Cascades</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://primer.ought.org/">ICE Primer Book</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://socraticmodels.github.io/">Socratic Models</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="memetic-proxy">
<h2>Memetic Proxy<a class="headerlink" href="#memetic-proxy" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Encouraging the LLM to respond in a certain way framing the discussion in a context that the model knows of and that will result in that type of response. For example, as a conversation between a student and a teacher.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2102.07350.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="self-consistency">
<h2>Self Consistency<a class="headerlink" href="#self-consistency" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A decoding strategy that samples a diverse set of reasoning paths and then selects the most consistent answer.
Is most effective when combined with Chain-of-thought prompting.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.11171.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="inception">
<h2>Inception<a class="headerlink" href="#inception" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Also called “First Person Instruction”.
Encouraging the model to think a certain way by including the start of the model’s response in the prompt.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://twitter.com/goodside/status/1583262455207460865?s=20&amp;t=8Hz7XBnK1OF8siQrxxCIGQ">Example</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="memprompt">
<h2>MemPrompt<a class="headerlink" href="#memprompt" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>MemPrompt maintains a memory of errors and user feedback, and uses them to prevent repetition of mistakes.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://memprompt.com/">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
</section>
</article>

<h1>Glossary</h1>
<!-- Table of contents -->
<div id="print-main-content">
<div id="jb-print-toc">
<div>
<h2> Contents </h2>
</div>
<nav aria-label="Page">
<ul class="visible nav section-nav flex-column">
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#chain-of-thought-prompting">Chain of Thought Prompting</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#action-plan-generation">Action Plan Generation</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#react-prompting">ReAct Prompting</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#self-ask">Self-ask</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#prompt-chaining">Prompt Chaining</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#memetic-proxy">Memetic Proxy</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#self-consistency">Self Consistency</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#inception">Inception</a></li>

</ul>

<article class="bd-article" role="main">
<section id="glossary">
<h1>Glossary<a class="headerlink" href="#glossary" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h1>
<p>This is a collection of terminology commonly used when developing LLM applications.
It contains reference to external papers or sources where the concept was first introduced,
as well as to places in LangChain where the concept is used.</p>
<section id="chain-of-thought-prompting">
<h2>Chain of Thought Prompting<a class="headerlink" href="#chain-of-thought-prompting" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting technique used to encourage the model to generate a series of intermediate reasoning steps.
A less formal way to induce this behavior is to include “Let’s think step-by-step” in the prompt.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2201.11903.pdf">Chain-of-Thought Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.00114">Step-by-Step Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="action-plan-generation">
<h2>Action Plan Generation<a class="headerlink" href="#action-plan-generation" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompt usage that uses a language model to generate actions to take.
The results of these actions can then be fed back into the language model to generate a subsequent action.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2112.09332.pdf">WebGPT Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://say-can.github.io/assets/palm_saycan.pdf">SayCan Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="react-prompting">
<h2>ReAct Prompting<a class="headerlink" href="#react-prompting" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting technique that combines Chain-of-Thought prompting with action plan generation.
This induces the to model to think about what action to take, then take it.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2210.03629.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference internal" href="modules/agents/agents/examples/react.html"><span class="doc std std-doc">LangChain Example</span></a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="self-ask">
<h2>Self-ask<a class="headerlink" href="#self-ask" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting method that builds on top of chain-of-thought prompting.
In this method, the model explicitly asks itself follow-up questions, which are then answered by an external search engine.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://ofir.io/self-ask.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference internal" href="modules/agents/agents/examples/self_ask_with_search.html"><span class="doc std std-doc">LangChain Example</span></a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="prompt-chaining">
<h2>Prompt Chaining<a class="headerlink" href="#prompt-chaining" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Combining multiple LLM calls together, with the output of one-step being the input to the next.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.06566.pdf">PromptChainer Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.10342">Language Model Cascades</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://primer.ought.org/">ICE Primer Book</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://socraticmodels.github.io/">Socratic Models</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="memetic-proxy">
<h2>Memetic Proxy<a class="headerlink" href="#memetic-proxy" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Encouraging the LLM to respond in a certain way framing the discussion in a context that the model knows of and that will result in that type of response. For example, as a conversation between a student and a teacher.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2102.07350.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="self-consistency">
<h2>Self Consistency<a class="headerlink" href="#self-consistency" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A decoding strategy that samples a diverse set of reasoning paths and then selects the most consistent answer.
Is most effective when combined with Chain-of-thought prompting.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.11171.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="inception">
<h2>Inception<a class="headerlink" href="#inception" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Also called “First Person Instruction”.
Encouraging the model to think a certain way by including the start of the model’s response in the prompt.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://twitter.com/goodside/status/1583262455207460865?s=20&amp;t=8Hz7XBnK1OF8siQrxxCIGQ">Example</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="memprompt">
<h2>MemPrompt<a class="headerlink" href="#memprompt" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>MemPrompt maintains a memory of errors and user feedback, and uses them to prevent repetition of mistakes.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://memprompt.com/">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
</section>
</article>

<h1>Glossary</h1>
<!-- Table of contents -->
<div id="print-main-content">
<div id="jb-print-toc">
<div>
<h2> Contents </h2>
</div>
<nav aria-label="Page">
<ul class="visible nav section-nav flex-column">
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#chain-of-thought-prompting">Chain of Thought Prompting</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#action-plan-generation">Action Plan Generation</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#react-prompting">ReAct Prompting</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#self-ask">Self-ask</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#prompt-chaining">Prompt Chaining</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#memetic-proxy">Memetic Proxy</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#self-consistency">Self Consistency</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#inception">Inception</a></li>

</ul>

<article class="bd-article" role="main">
<section id="glossary">
<h1>Glossary<a class="headerlink" href="#glossary" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h1>
<p>This is a collection of terminology commonly used when developing LLM applications.
It contains reference to external papers or sources where the concept was first introduced,
as well as to places in LangChain where the concept is used.</p>
<section id="chain-of-thought-prompting">
<h2>Chain of Thought Prompting<a class="headerlink" href="#chain-of-thought-prompting" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting technique used to encourage the model to generate a series of intermediate reasoning steps.
A less formal way to induce this behavior is to include “Let’s think step-by-step” in the prompt.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2201.11903.pdf">Chain-of-Thought Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.00114">Step-by-Step Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="action-plan-generation">
<h2>Action Plan Generation<a class="headerlink" href="#action-plan-generation" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompt usage that uses a language model to generate actions to take.
The results of these actions can then be fed back into the language model to generate a subsequent action.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2112.09332.pdf">WebGPT Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://say-can.github.io/assets/palm_saycan.pdf">SayCan Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="react-prompting">
<h2>ReAct Prompting<a class="headerlink" href="#react-prompting" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting technique that combines Chain-of-Thought prompting with action plan generation.
This induces the to model to think about what action to take, then take it.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2210.03629.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference internal" href="modules/agents/agents/examples/react.html"><span class="doc std std-doc">LangChain Example</span></a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="self-ask">
<h2>Self-ask<a class="headerlink" href="#self-ask" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting method that builds on top of chain-of-thought prompting.
In this method, the model explicitly asks itself follow-up questions, which are then answered by an external search engine.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://ofir.io/self-ask.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference internal" href="modules/agents/agents/examples/self_ask_with_search.html"><span class="doc std std-doc">LangChain Example</span></a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="prompt-chaining">
<h2>Prompt Chaining<a class="headerlink" href="#prompt-chaining" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Combining multiple LLM calls together, with the output of one-step being the input to the next.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.06566.pdf">PromptChainer Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.10342">Language Model Cascades</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://primer.ought.org/">ICE Primer Book</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://socraticmodels.github.io/">Socratic Models</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="memetic-proxy">
<h2>Memetic Proxy<a class="headerlink" href="#memetic-proxy" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Encouraging the LLM to respond in a certain way framing the discussion in a context that the model knows of and that will result in that type of response. For example, as a conversation between a student and a teacher.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2102.07350.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="self-consistency">
<h2>Self Consistency<a class="headerlink" href="#self-consistency" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A decoding strategy that samples a diverse set of reasoning paths and then selects the most consistent answer.
Is most effective when combined with Chain-of-thought prompting.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.11171.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="inception">
<h2>Inception<a class="headerlink" href="#inception" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Also called “First Person Instruction”.
Encouraging the model to think a certain way by including the start of the model’s response in the prompt.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://twitter.com/goodside/status/1583262455207460865?s=20&amp;t=8Hz7XBnK1OF8siQrxxCIGQ">Example</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="memprompt">
<h2>MemPrompt<a class="headerlink" href="#memprompt" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>MemPrompt maintains a memory of errors and user feedback, and uses them to prevent repetition of mistakes.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://memprompt.com/">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
</section>
</article>

<ul class="nav bd-sidenav">

<ul class="nav bd-sidenav">
<li class="toctree-l1 has-children"><a class="reference internal" href="modules/models.html">Models</a><input class="toctree-checkbox" id="toctree-checkbox-1" name="toctree-checkbox-1" type="checkbox"/><label class="toctree-toggle" for="toctree-checkbox-1"><i class="fa-solid fa-chevron-down"></i></label><ul>

<h1>Glossary</h1>
<!-- Table of contents -->
<div id="print-main-content">
<div id="jb-print-toc">
<div>
<h2> Contents </h2>
</div>
<nav aria-label="Page">
<ul class="visible nav section-nav flex-column">
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#chain-of-thought-prompting">Chain of Thought Prompting</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#action-plan-generation">Action Plan Generation</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#react-prompting">ReAct Prompting</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#self-ask">Self-ask</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#prompt-chaining">Prompt Chaining</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#memetic-proxy">Memetic Proxy</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#self-consistency">Self Consistency</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#inception">Inception</a></li>

</ul>

<article class="bd-article" role="main">
<section id="glossary">
<h1>Glossary<a class="headerlink" href="#glossary" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h1>
<p>This is a collection of terminology commonly used when developing LLM applications.
It contains reference to external papers or sources where the concept was first introduced,
as well as to places in LangChain where the concept is used.</p>
<section id="chain-of-thought-prompting">
<h2>Chain of Thought Prompting<a class="headerlink" href="#chain-of-thought-prompting" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting technique used to encourage the model to generate a series of intermediate reasoning steps.
A less formal way to induce this behavior is to include “Let’s think step-by-step” in the prompt.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2201.11903.pdf">Chain-of-Thought Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.00114">Step-by-Step Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="action-plan-generation">
<h2>Action Plan Generation<a class="headerlink" href="#action-plan-generation" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompt usage that uses a language model to generate actions to take.
The results of these actions can then be fed back into the language model to generate a subsequent action.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2112.09332.pdf">WebGPT Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://say-can.github.io/assets/palm_saycan.pdf">SayCan Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="react-prompting">
<h2>ReAct Prompting<a class="headerlink" href="#react-prompting" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting technique that combines Chain-of-Thought prompting with action plan generation.
This induces the to model to think about what action to take, then take it.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2210.03629.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference internal" href="modules/agents/agents/examples/react.html"><span class="doc std std-doc">LangChain Example</span></a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="self-ask">
<h2>Self-ask<a class="headerlink" href="#self-ask" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting method that builds on top of chain-of-thought prompting.
In this method, the model explicitly asks itself follow-up questions, which are then answered by an external search engine.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://ofir.io/self-ask.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference internal" href="modules/agents/agents/examples/self_ask_with_search.html"><span class="doc std std-doc">LangChain Example</span></a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="prompt-chaining">
<h2>Prompt Chaining<a class="headerlink" href="#prompt-chaining" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Combining multiple LLM calls together, with the output of one-step being the input to the next.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.06566.pdf">PromptChainer Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.10342">Language Model Cascades</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://primer.ought.org/">ICE Primer Book</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://socraticmodels.github.io/">Socratic Models</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="memetic-proxy">
<h2>Memetic Proxy<a class="headerlink" href="#memetic-proxy" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Encouraging the LLM to respond in a certain way framing the discussion in a context that the model knows of and that will result in that type of response. For example, as a conversation between a student and a teacher.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2102.07350.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="self-consistency">
<h2>Self Consistency<a class="headerlink" href="#self-consistency" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A decoding strategy that samples a diverse set of reasoning paths and then selects the most consistent answer.
Is most effective when combined with Chain-of-thought prompting.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.11171.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="inception">
<h2>Inception<a class="headerlink" href="#inception" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Also called “First Person Instruction”.
Encouraging the model to think a certain way by including the start of the model’s response in the prompt.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://twitter.com/goodside/status/1583262455207460865?s=20&amp;t=8Hz7XBnK1OF8siQrxxCIGQ">Example</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="memprompt">
<h2>MemPrompt<a class="headerlink" href="#memprompt" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>MemPrompt maintains a memory of errors and user feedback, and uses them to prevent repetition of mistakes.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://memprompt.com/">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
</section>
</article>

<div class="onlyprint" id="jb-print-docs-body">
<h1>Glossary</h1>
<!-- Table of contents -->
<div id="print-main-content">
<div id="jb-print-toc">
<div>
<h2> Contents </h2>
</div>
<nav aria-label="Page">
<ul class="visible nav section-nav flex-column">
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#chain-of-thought-prompting">Chain of Thought Prompting</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#action-plan-generation">Action Plan Generation</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#react-prompting">ReAct Prompting</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#self-ask">Self-ask</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#prompt-chaining">Prompt Chaining</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#memetic-proxy">Memetic Proxy</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#self-consistency">Self Consistency</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#inception">Inception</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#memprompt">MemPrompt</a></li>
</ul>

<article class="bd-article" role="main">
<section id="glossary">
<h1>Glossary<a class="headerlink" href="#glossary" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h1>
<p>This is a collection of terminology commonly used when developing LLM applications.
It contains reference to external papers or sources where the concept was first introduced,
as well as to places in LangChain where the concept is used.</p>
<section id="chain-of-thought-prompting">
<h2>Chain of Thought Prompting<a class="headerlink" href="#chain-of-thought-prompting" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting technique used to encourage the model to generate a series of intermediate reasoning steps.
A less formal way to induce this behavior is to include “Let’s think step-by-step” in the prompt.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2201.11903.pdf">Chain-of-Thought Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.00114">Step-by-Step Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="action-plan-generation">
<h2>Action Plan Generation<a class="headerlink" href="#action-plan-generation" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompt usage that uses a language model to generate actions to take.
The results of these actions can then be fed back into the language model to generate a subsequent action.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2112.09332.pdf">WebGPT Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://say-can.github.io/assets/palm_saycan.pdf">SayCan Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="react-prompting">
<h2>ReAct Prompting<a class="headerlink" href="#react-prompting" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting technique that combines Chain-of-Thought prompting with action plan generation.
This induces the to model to think about what action to take, then take it.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2210.03629.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference internal" href="modules/agents/agents/examples/react.html"><span class="doc std std-doc">LangChain Example</span></a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="self-ask">
<h2>Self-ask<a class="headerlink" href="#self-ask" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A prompting method that builds on top of chain-of-thought prompting.
In this method, the model explicitly asks itself follow-up questions, which are then answered by an external search engine.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://ofir.io/self-ask.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference internal" href="modules/agents/agents/examples/self_ask_with_search.html"><span class="doc std std-doc">LangChain Example</span></a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="prompt-chaining">
<h2>Prompt Chaining<a class="headerlink" href="#prompt-chaining" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Combining multiple LLM calls together, with the output of one-step being the input to the next.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.06566.pdf">PromptChainer Paper</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.10342">Language Model Cascades</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://primer.ought.org/">ICE Primer Book</a></p></li>
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://socraticmodels.github.io/">Socratic Models</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="memetic-proxy">
<h2>Memetic Proxy<a class="headerlink" href="#memetic-proxy" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Encouraging the LLM to respond in a certain way framing the discussion in a context that the model knows of and that will result in that type of response. For example, as a conversation between a student and a teacher.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2102.07350.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="self-consistency">
<h2>Self Consistency<a class="headerlink" href="#self-consistency" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>A decoding strategy that samples a diverse set of reasoning paths and then selects the most consistent answer.
Is most effective when combined with Chain-of-thought prompting.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.11171.pdf">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="inception">
<h2>Inception<a class="headerlink" href="#inception" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>Also called “First Person Instruction”.
Encouraging the model to think a certain way by including the start of the model’s response in the prompt.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://twitter.com/goodside/status/1583262455207460865?s=20&amp;t=8Hz7XBnK1OF8siQrxxCIGQ">Example</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="memprompt">
<h2>MemPrompt<a class="headerlink" href="#memprompt" title="Permalink to this headline">#</a></h2>
<p>MemPrompt maintains a memory of errors and user feedback, and uses them to prevent repetition of mistakes.</p>
<p>Resources:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://memprompt.com/">Paper</a></p></li>
</ul>
</section>
</section>
</article>

<h1>Glossary</h1>
<!-- Table of contents -->
<div id="print-main-content">
<div id="jb-print-toc">
<div>
<h2> Contents </h2>
</div>
<nav aria-label="Page">
<ul class="visible nav section-nav flex-column">
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#chain-of-thought-prompting">Chain of Thought Prompting</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#action-plan-generation">Action Plan Generation</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#react-prompting">ReAct Prompting</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#self-ask">Self-ask</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#prompt-chaining">Prompt Chaining</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#memetic-proxy">Memetic Proxy</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#self-consistency">Self Consistency</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#inception">Inception</a></li>
<li class="toc-h2 nav-item toc-entry"><a class="reference internal nav-link" href="#memprompt">MemPrompt</a></li>
</ul>
